title: blechnum tomentosum
intro: Blechnum tomentosum, also known as the woolly chain fern or hairy chain fern, is a species of fern native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia, characterized by its hairy rhizome and fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Blechnum tomentosum include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. Blechnum tomentosum is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
